proved: A scenario record has exact schema/version, stable identity, title, background, draft/ready/archived state, optional parent, creator attribution, ordered turns, and attributed scenario edits.
Each turn has stable identity/creator data plus immutable attributed revisions; all concurrent alternatives remain and a deterministic timestamp/edit/author ordering selects the current projection.
Lifecycle create/read/update/delete, turn add/update/delete, branch creation, and multi-turn role/content round-trip work in the domain harness.
Independent concurrent scenario field edits and ordered turn additions converge with both attributions across forty duplicate/reordered deliveries.
Top-level scenario deletion remains authoritative over a concurrent nested turn revision across another forty duplicate/reordered deliveries.
Peer-specific internal keys preserve disconnected public scenario, turn, and edit identity collisions so merged projection fails closed rather than silently selecting one.
Exact record/turn/revision schemas, order-to-map consistency, bounds, unknown fields, missing parents, and cyclic ancestry are validated or reported.
P-14/P-15 are implemented_unverified; no gallery UI, model generation, evaluation, voting, response, or portable-pack claim is made.
notProved: A visible scenario gallery, editor, status control, keyboard/pointer workflow, accessibility, or themes.
Scenario generation/regeneration, response variants, examples, votes, flags, notes, labels, evaluation, or policy spotlighting.
Turn reordering/moving, concurrent move-vs-edit semantics, or large-scenario performance.
Authenticated participant identity or a trusted distributed clock; winner metadata is caller-supplied.
Portable scenario-pack/archive export or import; the test round-trip is a Yjs provider update only.
Two-machine Drive/WebSocket collaboration, presence, authorization, or tamper recovery UI.
